description_long: This is a vintage folding bellows camera, likely from the early to mid-20th century, featuring a textured, dark leatherette exterior and a metal frame. The front of the camera displays a circular lens assembly with visible dials and markings, surrounded by a metal support structure. The bellows, made of pleated material, extend from the lens to the main body, allowing for adjustable focal length. The camera has a carrying handle on top and a metal latch mechanism on the side for securing the folding body. The back and sides are covered in a textured material, with a small round window on the rear for viewing film status. The overall appearance is worn, with visible scuffs and signs of age, suggesting it has seen significant use.
description: A vintage folding bellows camera with a textured leatherette exterior, metal frame, and extendable pleated bellows. The front features a circular lens assembly and visible dials, while the body shows signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lens assembly, extended bellows, and metal support structure, with the camera's nameplate visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the extended bellows, the metal side latch, and the carrying handle on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed back panel with a small round window and the textured leatherette covering.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the folded bellows, side latch, and the overall boxy shape of the camera body.
